_provenance: MACHINE-CAPTURED 2026-07-17 via edgartools==5.42.0, edgar.Company('AAPL').get_filings(form="10-K") / get_filings(form="10-Q"); accession/filing_date/period_of_report dumped exactly as returned, never hand-typed. AAPL's FYE is 2025-09-27 (September) -- a NON-DECEMBER fiscal year end. FY2025's Q1 period_of_report (2024-12-28) falls in CALENDAR year 2024, one year off from its FISCAL year (2025). This is exactly the case that exposed the calendar-vs-fiscal quarterly-coverage grouping bug (Task 10 revision round 1, reviewer's live probe on AAPL/NVDA). fy2026_q1/fy2026_q2 (added revision round 2) are AAPL's real IN-PROGRESS FY2026 10-Qs -- captured with NO FY2026 10-K on file yet (not due until ~Oct 2026), exposing the calendar-year-fallback regression (round 2 fatal finding): fy2026_q1's period_of_report (2025-12-27) shares CALENDAR year 2025 with FY2025's own filings, so the pre-fix fallback misfiled it into FY2025's bucket instead of projecting FY2026's fiscal calendar forward. Capture command: `edgar.set_identity(...); c = edgar.Company('AAPL'); sorted(c.get_filings(form='10-K'), key=lambda f: f.filing_date, reverse=True)[:2]` and the analogous `form='10-Q'` call (top 5), reading `.accession_no / .filing_date / .period_of_report` off each real Filing.
